prior_claim_promotion_blockers: N15 AP5 proxy formation is not AP6 boundary separability by relabel

boundary_rows.rationale: artifact basin-boundary separability is not semantic or phenomenological selfhood
boundary-side assignment is an artifact descriptor, not identity acceptance
internal support-relevant state is not goal ownership or goal understanding
boundary-crossing trace is not a closed action-perception loop
artifact-level AP6 classification does not open native support or Phase 8
artifact-level boundary evidence is not fully native integration
external resource or challenge descriptors are not an agency environment model
B3_C4 is artifact-level reclosure evidence, not autonomous repair
B4_C5 is artifact-level shared-medium separability evidence only
N16 excludes selective uptake, resource assimilation, organism, and life claims
duplicate replay is an I7 run-level replay extension, not an I2 schema-backed control

claim_boundary_record.global_stress_envelope_scope_note: Global aggregate metrics are computed across every reused I3-I6 row, including null, rejected, and partial control rows. Supported boundary-candidate metrics exclude B0 active-null rows and unsupported rows.

artifact_level_ap6_supported.reason: I8 classifies artifact-level AP6 as supported while final AP6 freeze and unsafe promotions remain false

boundary_drift_outside_policy_control.attack: let boundary drift outside frozen policy

duplicate_replay_control.attack: rerun I7 synthesis over identical serialized inputs
duplicate_replay_control.schema_status: i7_full_control_extension_not_i2_schema_backed; replay_matrix contains the authoritative duplicate digest comparison

hidden_external_state_injection_control.attack: derive support from unrecorded external state

identity_acceptance_relabel_control.attack: relabel side assignment as identity acceptance

missing_boundary_side_state_control.attack: accept a row without both side assignments when required

multi_basin_merge_control.attack: treat neighbor leakage or merge pressure as separability

native_support_relabel_control.attack: promote artifact-level boundary support to native support

post_hoc_boundary_label_control.attack: apply boundary label after seeing successful rows

resource_relabel_as_self_control.attack: relabel external resource or medium as self-region

self_support_relabel_as_external_control.attack: move support-relevant internal state to the external side

selfhood_personhood_relabel_control.attack: promote artifact boundary to selfhood or personhood

semantic_goal_ownership_relabel_control.attack: promote AP5/AP6 artifacts to semantic goal ownership

stale_external_state_control.attack: use stale external challenge or medium state

structured_external_coherence_rejection_.attack: promote coherent external structure to boundary support

untracked_boundary_crossing_control.attack: accept a boundary crossing without boundary trace

artifact_only_replay.meaning: I7 uses serialized I3-I6 artifacts and configs only

duplicate_replay.meaning: same accepted rows and requirement synthesis from same artifact inputs

order_inversion_replay.meaning: row order does not create boundary evidence

snapshot_load_replay.meaning: serialized row state can be loaded and re-evaluated without runtime mutation

whole_experiment_interpretation: N16 now has a claim-clean artifact-level AP6 classification: internal support-relevant state and external resource, perturbation, structured-state, and shared-medium pressures are separable in the generated artifacts and controls. The result remains a basin-boundary candidate pending Iteration 9 closeout; it does not support selfhood, identity acceptance, semantic goal ownership, agency, native support, Phase 8, fully native integration, selective uptake, resource assimilation, organism claims, life claims, or a closed action-perception loop.
